danger_signals.visual_form: Vehicle sprite interaction with roadside objects or other cars
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate hazard requiring avoidance; collision causes vehicle flip and crash
Checkpoint deadline approaching; failure to reach checkered flag before time expires ends stage

danger_signals.visual_form: Opposing vehicle sprites scaling up and moving toward player lane
danger_signals.meaning: Increasing difficulty; rival cars become faster and more challenging as stages progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Speedometer needle dropping below optimal racing speed
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of momentum; inability to maintain pace risks missing checkpoints within time limit

safety_signals.visual_form: Checkered flag sprite appearing on road ahead
safety_signals.meaning: Checkpoint reached; adds time and progression marker; visible navigation goal

safety_signals.visual_form: Unobstructed sprite-based road environment with minimal traffic
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage available; opportunity to accelerate and build speed

safety_signals.visual_form: Speed transition from normal (100 km/h) to turbo mode (up to 255 km/h)
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced capability state; acceleration boost enables faster stage completion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Selectable car sprite (328 Twin Turbo or F1 Machine)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Both vehicle options perform identically; no competitive differentiation through car type
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Always viewed from behind-vehicle perspective; centered in screen frame

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Multiple opponent car sprites (Volkswagen Beetles to Ferrari Testarossas) scaling in visual size and speed
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse vehicle types represent increasing difficulty tiers across stage progression
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear in traffic lanes ahead of player; sprite scaling indicates relative distance and threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct skyline and environment graphics for each of eight stages (Key West, Big Apple, Gettysburg, Monument Valley, Las Vegas Blvd., Rocky Mountains, Twilight California, Bay Bridge)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Road layout, obstacle placement, and competitor density vary by stage; difficulty escalates progressively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dashboard time indicator visually depletes; coasting and deceleration sequence triggers when time expires
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unlike Out Run's immediate game-end, player vehicle decelerates gradually (5-10 second buffer), extending stage window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent vehicle sprites appear with increasing fidelity and speed as stages advance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicle performance tiers escalate from early-stage rivals to high-performance competitors; speed delta increases challenge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stereoscopic 3D effect available via anaglyph glasses or Famicom 3D shutter glasses; scanline-based road perspective enhances depth
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional enhancement; gameplay mechanics remain identical; provides immersive sense of speed and forward momentum

environmental_storytelling.example: Roadside billboards designed by developer Takashi Tokita convey location context and sponsorship atmosphere within each stage environment
Distinct visual backdrops for eight stages (Los Angeles, San Francisco, Athens referenced) establish geographic progression of 'Transamerica' race from West Coast to East Coast
Vehicle type variety (from Volkswagen Beetles to Ferrari Testarossas) reflects stage-specific economic and geographic character

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Stage-specific obstacle placement and road curvature communicate environmental hazards and navigation challenge

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Accelerates to maximum 100 km/h; controllable via brake, steer, and turbo activation
Accelerates to maximum 255 km/h; dramatically increases pace and stage completion capability
Vehicle flips and crashes; stage progression interrupted
Coasts and decelerates from current speed to 0 km/h over 5-10 second window; allows brief additional checkpoint pursuit opportunity
Slower performance; manageable threat level; Volkswagen Beetles and lower-tier vehicles
Increased speed; Ferrari Testarossas and high-performance rivals; heightened traffic density and collision risk
Marks checkpoint or stage completion; interaction triggers time addition or stage end event

notes: Rad Racer II is a sprite-based racing game with limited faction complexity; danger and safety signals are primarily mechanical rather than narrative. Visual communication relies heavily on dashboard instrumentation (speedometer, tachometer, time remaining, progress bar) and environmental contrast. The game's primary world-state manifestation is geographic progression across eight distinct stages, each with unique skyline and obstacle characteristics. No explicit political or factional systems documented. The 3D stereoscopic mode represents an optional environmental enhancement rather than a world-state mechanic. Vehicle selection (328 Twin Turbo vs. F1 Machine) is cosmetic, offering no gameplay differentiation.
